test-set.name: prod-RecordType
test-set.description: Tests for RecordType item type (proposed 4.0 extension)

test-case.name: RecordType-001
test-case.description: instance of test, no declared types
test-case.test: map{"x":3, "y":4} instance of record(x, y)

test-case.name: RecordType-002
test-case.description: Use in type signature

test-case.name: RecordType-003
test-case.description: Record types with absent field type

test-case.name: RecordType-004
test-case.description: Fields in record type may be omitted if optional
test-case.test: map{"name":"xyz"} instance of record(name as xs:string, value? as item()?)

test-case.name: RecordType-005
test-case.description: Fields in record type may be empty
test-case.test: map{"name":"xyz", "value":()} instance of record(name as xs:string, value as item()?)

test-case.name: RecordType-006
test-case.description: Undeclared fields in record type may not be present, unless the type is extensible
test-case.test: map{"name":1, "value":(), "extra":3} instance of record(name as xs:string, value as item()?)

test-case.name: RecordType-007
test-case.description: Fields in extensible record type may be present even if undeclared
test-case.test: map{"name":1, "value":(), "extra":3} instance of record(name as xs:numeric, value as item()?, *)

test-case.name: RecordType-008
test-case.description: Fields in record type may not be omitted unless the field is optional
test-case.test: map{"name":"xyz"} instance of record(name as item()*, value as item()?)

test-case.name: RecordType-009
test-case.description: Fields in non-extensible record type may not be present if undeclared
test-case.test: map{"name":1, "value":(), "extra":3} instance of record(name as xs:string, value as item()*)

test-case.name: RecordType-010
test-case.description: Coercion happens
test-case.test: (function($x as record(size as xs:double)) as xs:double {$x?size + 1})(map{'size':12})

test-case.name: RecordType-011
test-case.description: Coercion to a record type changes order of fields
test-case.test: let $k := function($x as record(a, b, c, d)) as xs:string* {map:keys($x)} return $k({'c':(), 'a':(), 'b':(), 'd':()})

test-case.name: RecordType-012
test-case.description: Coercion to a record type changes order of fields
test-case.test: let $k := function($x as record(a, b, d, c, *)) as xs:string* {map:keys($x)} return $k({'e':(), 'c':(), 'a':(), 'g':(), 'b':(), 'd':()})

test-case.name: RecordType-013
test-case.description: Coercion to a record type changes order of fields
test-case.test: let $k := function($x as record(a, b, d?, c, *)) as xs:string* {map:keys($x)} return $k({'e':(), 'c':(), 'a':(), 'g':(), 'b':()})

test-case.name: RecordType-014
test-case.description: Coercion to a record type changes order of fields
test-case.test: let $k := function($x as record(a as xs:int, b as xs:int, d? as xs:int, c as xs:int, *)) as xs:string* {map:keys($x)} return $k({'e':0, 'c':0, 'a':0, 'g':0, 'b':0})

test-case.name: RecordType-015
test-case.description: Coercion changes values
test-case.test: let $k := function($x as record(a as xs:int, b as xs:int, d? as xs:int, c as xs:int)) as item()* {$x?*} return $k({'c':0, 'a':0, 'b':0})

test-case.name: RecordType-016
test-case.description: Coercion drops excess fields
test-case.test: let $k := function($x as record(a as xs:int, b as xs:int)) as item()* {count(map:keys($x))} return $k({'c':0, 'a':0, 'b':0})

test-case.name: RecordType-017
test-case.description: Coercion drops excess fields
test-case.test: let $k := function($x as record(a as xs:int, b as xs:int, c? as xs:int?)) as item()* {count(map:keys($x))} return $k({'d':0, 'a':0, 'b':0})

test-case.name: RecordType-018
test-case.description: Coercion drops excess fields
test-case.test: let $k := function($x as record(a as xs:int, b as xs:int, c? as xs:int?)) as item()* {count(map:keys($x))} return $k({'d':0, 'a':0, 'b':0})

test-case.name: RecordType-103
test-case.description: record(*) is now allowed
test-case.test: map{} instance of record(*)

test-case.name: RecordType-104
test-case.description: record(*) is now allowed
test-case.test: map{'a': 22} instance of record(*)

test-case.name: RecordType-105
test-case.description: record() is now allowed
test-case.test: map{} instance of record()

test-case.name: RecordType-106
test-case.description: record() is now allowed
test-case.test: map{'a':1} instance of record()
